channel.title: Observability on OpenSwap Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Observability on OpenSwap Guide

item.title: Best Self-Hosted eBPF Networking & Observability Tools: Cilium vs Pixie vs Tetragon 2026
item.description: eBPF (extended Berkeley Packet Filter) has fundamentally changed how we observe, secure, and manage Linux networking and applications. By running sandboxed programs directly inside the kernel, eBPF tools can intercept system calls, parse network packets, and trace function executions with near-zero overhead — no agents, no sidecars, no kernel module recompilation.

item.title: Self-Hosted Datadog Alternative: SigNoz vs Grafana Stack vs HyperDX 2026
item.description: Commercial observability platforms like Datadog, New Relic, and AppDynamics have become the default choice for monitoring modern applications. But their pricing models — often based on host count, data ingestion volume, or custom metric cardinality — can spiral into thousands of dollars per month as your infrastructure grows.
